handwaver_inspired_build_by_stretching: HandWaver-inspired: build by stretching point → line → plane → solid
sculpt_mode_title: AI Sculpt: describe an object, the AI builds it from primitives — then refine it
enter_vr_title: Enter VR — stand next to your model (needs a headset)
click_ai_tutor_or_press_to_get_insight: Click "AI Tutor" or press / to get insights about the current shape.
ai_sculpt_help: Describe an object — the AI designs it from primitive shapes (box, sphere, cylinder, cone, torus). Then refine it by hand or by asking.
ai_sculpt_no_ai: AI Sculpt needs the Gemini API.
sculpt_placeholder: e.g. a rocket ship, a friendly robot…
voice_sculpt_title: Voice sculpt — speak to create and shape it, hands-free
sculpt_refine_placeholder: Tell the AI what to change…
build_geometry_by_stretching_a_point_i: Build geometry by stretching a point into a line, a line into a plane, and a plane into a solid. Each stretch adds a new object to the scene.
voice_build_title: Voice build — speak the stretches to build point to line to plane to solid, hands-free
mostly_points_stretch_any_to_begin_bui: Mostly points — stretch any to begin building line segments.
line_segments_dominate_stretching_them: Line segments dominate. Stretching them perpendicular yields planes.
rectangles_dominate_stretch_perpendicu: Rectangles dominate. Stretch perpendicular to a plane to extrude prisms.
solids_dominate_each_prism_has_interio: Solids dominate. Each prism has interior volume and surface boundary.
predict_geometry_properties_as_you_str: Predict geometry properties as you stretch. No answer key — you mark your own understanding.
your_hypothesis_what_stretches_grow_le: Your hypothesis (what stretches grow length faster — area faster — volume faster?)
e_g_stretching_a_segment_perpendicular: e.g., stretching a segment perpendicular doubles area but volume needs a second stretch...
when_you_stretch_a_segment_perpendicul: When you stretch a segment perpendicular to itself, what happens to its dimension count?
if_centroid_shifts_after_a_stretch_wha: If centroid shifts after a stretch, what direction did you stretch most?
how_does_total_volume_change_vs_total_: How does total volume change vs total area as you stretch the same axis twice?
what_would_make_this_construction_symm: What would make this construction symmetric about its centroid?
i_can_explain_why_these_metrics_scale_: I can explain why these metrics scale the way they do as I stretch.
inquiry_widget_no_score_no_reveal_no_a: Inquiry widget — no score, no reveal, no answer dump.
euler_s_polyhedron_formula_for_any_con: Euler's polyhedron formula: for any convex polyhedron, V − E + F = 2
alloflow_requires_hardware_acceleratio: AlloFlow requires hardware acceleration to display 3D graphics. Please enable WebGL in your browser settings to interact with this lab.
drag_rotate_scroll_zoom_right_click_pa: 🖱️ Drag: rotate • Scroll: zoom • Right-click: pan
tip_your_answer_must_be_within_5_of_th: 💡 Tip: Your answer must be within 5% of the exact value. Use π ≈ 3.14159
stl_files_are_unit_less_most_3d_printe: 💡 STL files are unit-less. Most 3D printer slicers (Cura, PrusaSlicer) default to millimeters. A shape with width=5 will print as 5mm wide.
